title: Hot Water Funnel — Sales-Led Strategy
description: Sales-led strategy to own Gold Coast heat pump hot water demand through fixed-price positioning, Google Ads, local SEO, Google Business Profile, and a 90-day conversion system.
tldr: A fixed $3,600 standard-install offer gives the business a clearer sales message than broader plumbing competitors.
Estimated Gold Coast search demand sits around 1.5k–3k+ monthly across heat pump, replacement, rebate, and brand-intent searches.
The first 90 days should launch one connected system across Google Ads, local SEO, Google Business Profile, conversion pages, eligibility, booking, and deposits.
A specialist market position can outperform general local operators because the offer is easier to remember, trust, and choose.
The recommended package is the Full-Funnel Revenue Engine at $14,750 over 90 days, with full site coverage, daily GBP activity, ads, SEO, automation, and reporting.
At $3,600 per standard install, even 2–4 booked installs per week can generate $28.8k–$57.6k in monthly gross revenue before scaling higher.
The rollout should progress month by month: core pages and campaigns first, then broader local coverage, then compounding reviews, referrals, and optimisation.
Keep the public promise simple for standard installs and route non-standard jobs into manual review before final confirmation.

hero.eyebrow: Gold Coast heat pump hot water strategy
hero.headline: Gold Coast homeowners want heat pumps. They can't find you.
hero.subtitle: Gold Coast homeowners searching for heat pump hot water systems are finding your competitors first. This strategy shows exactly how to capture that demand.

sections.heading: The Problem
sections.subtitle: Gold Coast homeowners are actively searching for heat pump hot water solutions, but they are finding established competitors — not you.

narrativeProse.heading: Buyers are searching with urgency and you are not showing up
narrativeProse.body: When a hot water system fails, homeowners go straight to Google. They search for heat pump installers, government rebates, and brand comparisons. Right now, the results they see are dominated by established plumbing firms and national brands — not your fixed-price offer.

narrativeProse.heading: Generalist plumbers own the conversation by default
narrativeProse.body: Most Gold Coast heat pump installations are quoted by general plumbing businesses. They do not specialise, but they rank because they have more reviews, more pages, and longer search histories. A focused heat pump offer should beat them — but only if buyers can find it.

stackCards.title: The buyer already understands the offer
stackCards.content: Heat pump hot water has a cleaner buying story than many local trades offers: lower running costs, better efficiency, and a replacement decision that often arrives with urgency.

stackCards.title: The pricing can be positioned credibly
stackCards.content: Broader Australian installation benchmarks still leave room for a $3,600 standard-install offer to feel commercially believable rather than confusingly cheap or over-engineered.

sections.heading: The Opportunity
sections.eyebrow: Why Hot Water Funnel can win — and what it is worth
sections.subtitle: The market has significant unbranded demand and Hot Water Funnel does not need to outspend incumbents — it needs to show up first where buyers are making decisions.

narrativeProse.heading: Why Your Offer Can Win on the Gold Coast
narrativeProse.body: Your offer gives you a strong local angle: fixed price, strong savings story, and a buying decision that often arrives with urgency. Buyers already understand the category, the price can be positioned credibly, and the path from replacement pain to booked install is cleaner than in many other local service categories.

narrativeProse.heading: Commercial Upside
narrativeProse.body: At $3,600 per standard install, the economics become meaningful quickly once the funnel starts producing booked jobs consistently. The ranges below show what booked-install volume can look like in gross revenue terms as the channel moves from early traction into stronger weekly output.

stackCards.title: The buyer already understands the offer
stackCards.content: Heat pump hot water has a cleaner buying story than many local trades offers: lower running costs, better efficiency, and a replacement decision that often arrives with urgency.

stackCards.title: The pricing can be positioned credibly
stackCards.content: Broader Australian installation benchmarks still leave room for a $3,600 standard-install offer to feel commercially believable rather than confusingly cheap or over-engineered.

stackCards.title: A specialist brand can out-position generalists
stackCards.content: Most local competitors still present as broad plumbing or multi-trade businesses. A narrower specialist position makes your business easier to remember, easier to trust, and easier to choose.

sections.heading: The Plan
sections.subtitle: Three phases. Own the decision stage. Reinforce it everywhere buyers look. Then expand into the broader market.

sectionLead.takeaway: Step 1: We will launch one connected system across acquisition, local visibility, qualification, booking, and monthly optimisation. Step 2: The Full-Funnel Revenue Engine is the complete build. The other two options exist as reduced-scope downsells, not equal alternatives. Step 3: If the goal is to build the full engine properly, this is the 90-day commercial model to anchor on.

stackCards.title: Google Ads management
stackCards.content: We will set up and manage a paid acquisition layer that captures high-intent demand around replacement, rebate, heat pump, and brand-led searches.

stackCards.title: Website and landing pages
stackCards.content: We will build a conversion-led site structure around one clear offer, one clear CTA path, and pages that support both paid traffic and organic search visibility.

stackCards.title: Google Business Profile and local SEO
stackCards.content: We will build the local visibility layer through Google Business Profile optimisation, on-page local SEO, service-area relevance, and supporting trust signals.

bullets: no major relocation of the unit
no crane work or unusual access constraints

cta.headline: Launch the full revenue engine, not a cut-down version
cta.body: If the goal is to own the Gold Coast heat pump niche properly, the Full-Funnel Revenue Engine is the cleanest option: full website footprint, full paid search coverage, daily GBP activity, full automation, and a 90-day rollout built to start generating installs and compound from there.

failureBlock.heading: The cost of staying invisible
bullets: Every month without optimized pages is a month where homeowners choose heat pump installers they can actually find.
AI answer engines are forming their local recommendation patterns now — businesses absent today get locked out.
Competitors already ranking for Gold Coast heat pump queries will keep capturing the demand.
The longer you wait, the more expensive it becomes to break into a market that others already own.

successBlock.eyebrow: What 12 months from now looks like
successBlock.heading: You become the default heat pump installer on the Gold Coast
bullets: When Gold Coast homeowners search for heat pump hot water systems, your business shows up first in Google, Maps, and AI answers.
You own the local decision-stage conversation with pages that answer every buyer question.
Reviews, local citations, and community proof reinforce the same story everywhere homeowners look.
A defensible local organic channel that generates leads without ongoing ad spend.
